relevance.evidence: Pack addresses a coherent PM workflow task. Six skills form a documented chain from user research through delivery with explicit cross-references in each skill's analysis.

coverage.evidence: Covers Discover→Define→Prioritize→Deliver stages. Excludes strategic planning (OKR, market sizing) and stakeholder comms — intentional scoping, not gaps.

non_redundancy.evidence: Each member occupies a distinct phase with unique outputs. No two skills produce the same artifact. No alternatives edges within the pack.

workflow_coherence.evidence: Four chains_with edges (weights 0.85–0.92) with explicit bidirectional routing between stages. Interview synthesis → problem-statement → PRD → user stories forms a clean pipeline.

compatibility.evidence: All skills share markdown-based, phase-gated, evidence-dependent assumptions. No tool conflicts. One strengthens edge (interview synthesis → prioritization framework) documented.

conflict_control.evidence: No conflicts found in any pairwise comparison. Zero conflicts_with edges across all analyzed skill pairs in the source group.

evidence_quality.evidence: All six relation edges cite explicit analysis quotes with weights ≥ 0.75. Analysis confidence ranges from medium to high. All member versions pinned.

actionability.evidence: 5-stage workflow with clear entry/exit points. Interview synthesis is the natural entry; user stories are the natural exit. Phase gating with explicit routing prevents skipping stages.

freshness.evidence: All skill records from 2026-07-09 sync. Source snapshot is current. No stale version signals detected.

source_quality.evidence: Single source (product-on-purpose, Apache-2.0), verified license, public GitHub repo, high-confidence activation.

failure_modes.recommendation: Re-sync source before next evaluation cycle to ensure version pins remain valid.

The pack describes 'Discover→Define→Prioritize→Deliver' but omits competitive analysis and market sizing from the workflow. Consider whether these excluded skills could form a companion strategy pack.

recommendations.reason: Score 0.85 exceeds 0.78 publication threshold. No blocking failures. Six evidence-backed edges with solid cross-references.
After promotion, render public docs/skills/ page for this pack so human visitors can discover it.

The excluded skills (competitive-analysis, market-sizing, OKR-writer, stakeholder-briefings) have edges within the same source group. A 'PM Strategy Pack' could cover the strategic planning layer these excluded skills address.
